title: Error: failed to mount overlay for metacopy check with "nodev,metacopy=on" options: invalid argument
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
`Error: failed to mount overlay for metacopy check with "nodev,metacopy=on" options: invalid argument`
each time I try to run podman on a fresh installation on my vps fedora 32
-->




**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
Error: failed to mount overlay for metacopy check with "nodev,metacopy=on" options: invalid argument
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

this is last version on fedora repo

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
vps at provider of vps

comments.body: What OS and what kernel are you doing this with?  Looks like you have a /etc/containers/storage.conf that has metacopyup=on defined, but your kernel does not support it.

fedora 32 server.
kernel seems to be that one  : 
`4.15.0-99-generic`
Kernel is most likely too old. You should edit your storage.conf to set `metacopy=off`.

Is there a way to let podman ignore the metacopy setting if the kernel does not support it. This will at least prevent podman from failing even if metacopy is set in storage.conf, but the kernel does not support it?

This will allow for the same container image (for container in container scenario) to run on hosts which does and does not support metacopy?
Open a PR for containers storage to handle this situation. There already is a metacopy check, so it should be able to ignore if it does not support.

@rhatdan The tests were not running well in my machine, so have made the changes I thought might be right and have created a PR (https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/796). Please let me know if any more steps is required.

I'm getting the `Error: failed to mount overlay for metacopy check with "nodev,metacopy=on" options: invalid argument
` after the install. I opened the `/etc/containers/storage.conf` and changed the `on` to `off` but I still get the same error. 

Is it expected to get this error from a clean install and how can I fix this issue? Can I just comment out that entire line?

Environment: Ubuntu 18.04
```
root@ubuntu1804:~/workspace/skopeo# podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
18.04 is a very old kernel, which makes me suspicious, but there's not enough detail to be sure. Please open a fresh issue with all the details requested in the issue template.
